trivial_fixes.new_string: counts: Record<CountId, number>;               // kills, clears, harvests — drive quest advancement & bestiary tallies (NOT a separate player "achievements" feature; pillar achievement-JUMPS are recognized deeds, §2.16, not these raw counts)
trivial_fixes.rationale: 'achievements' as a player feature appears only in this comment and no achievements registry/screen/milestone exists; v1 milestones never include the feature, so this is loose shorthand. Reword kills the dangling reference.

trivial_fixes.old_string: `npm run build:itch` = `vite build` + zip `dist/`. itch.io serves the unzipped
trivial_fixes.new_string: `npm run build:itch` = `vite build` + zip **the *contents* of** `dist/` (so `index.html` sits at the **archive root**, never nested under a `dist/` folder — itch.io requires `index.html` at the zip root or the embed shows a blank frame). itch.io serves the unzipped
trivial_fixes.rationale: itch.io's HTML host needs index.html at the archive root; 'zip dist/' read literally nests it and yields a blank-frame upload. Factual clarification, no design judgement.

human_questions.question: Fix the two broken halves of the T0 economic proof. (A) The T0 Estate ≥0.8K gate proof spends 72/560 deed-ip on 'sealed trade contracts' at T0, but TRADE is a LOCKED T1 reveal ('no market in T0') — should T0 open a limited rice-sale/barter strand, or be re-itemized using only LAND/TREASURY deeds (e.g. +3 solvency @26 or +5 yield @16)? (B) Is the per-rung koku throughput GROSS or NET of food/re-investment? The doc uses both labels for the same 21.5K integral, implying incompatible held balances (~19K net vs 3-5K gross), and §4.7.5 proves E2@R6 affordability against lifetime-produced rather than held.
human_questions.context: These are the centrepiece T0 balance proofs that rounds 1-2 treated as sound. As itemized, the Estate gate reaches only 728<800, and the held-koku trajectory that affordability/soft-lock depend on is modelled nowhere — so a R6 soft-lock on the gating E2 build cannot be ruled out.
options: A-keep-trade-T1: re-itemize T0 Estate 560 with LAND/TREASURY deeds only; declare throughput GROSS and add an explicit per-rung food/tools price line + held-koku ledger, re-stating §4.7.5 affordability against held
A-open-T0-barter: add a limited T0 rice-sale strand (reconcile the 'no market in T0' lock); keep throughput NET and correct the held figure to ~19K
mixed: re-itemize without trade (cheap, preserves the lock) AND pick one koku accounting basis
human_questions.recommendation: Re-itemize the T0 Estate 560 with LAND/TREASURY deeds (preserves the locked 'no market in T0'), and declare throughput GROSS + author a minimal held-koku ledger so E2@R6 affordability is verified from held — both are needed to close the soft-lock risk.

human_questions.question: How is an estate-ladder rung promotion gated? Pick ONE: (A) numeric meter — rungs flip when a stored estateService/combatStanding meter crosses a threshold, requiring §4 to add the accrual rule (what increments it, by how much, on the 'steep geometric' curve) and a per-rung threshold table; or (B) event/flag-gated — rungs flip on the §3.2.1 narrative triggers (spilled-rice done, surviving the wolf, returning the ledger) already expressible as flags, with the stored meters becoming derived/cosmetic.
human_questions.context: M1 phase 4 requires the meter to accrue from recognized labour, cross R0→R1→R2, and be Vitest-asserted at the intended GameState — currently unwritable. §3.2.1 supplies codeable narrative triggers, so R0→R1 is implementable today; the missing piece is the meter accrual law + numeric thresholds the prose references.
options: A-numeric-meter: add accrual rule + per-rung threshold table to §4 (introduces a third quantified curve to balance)
B-flag-gated: gate on §3.2.1 flags/counts; redefine estateService/combatStanding as derived display values and drop the 'thresholds' wording in §2.15(c)
human_questions.recommendation: B (flag/event-gated) for T0 — the §3.2.1 triggers map straight to flags, nothing new to balance, and M1 phase 4's descriptions are already flag-shaped; keep the meters as a smoothed display.

human_questions.question: Specify the soft-stamina/satiety system and whether it throttles combat. (A) Magnitudes: the rate-multiplier curve as a function of satiety %, its non-zero floor, per-action satiety drain + default staminaCost per node, and rest-recovery per tick. (B) Scope: does low satiety throttle combat (add a satiety term to §4.6.1 — which stat(s), enemy symmetry, floor; may shift the LOCKED 20-35% first-fight win-rate) or is the throttle labour-only with satiety merely draining in combat?
human_questions.context: §4.7.1's yield formula multiplies by an undefined '(soft-stamina rate)' and M1 phases 2-3 must implement + test it; §2.3(c)/§6.3 say the throttle hits 'labour/combat speed' but §4.6.1 has no satiety term while §4.7.1 does.
options: gentle-labour-only: shallow curve, high floor (~0.7, bites <40%); throttle scoped to labour, combat satiety-independent (preserves locked combat curve)
steep-with-combat: lower floor (~0.3), and add a combat satiety term (strengthens 'eat before you fight' but perturbs the locked first-fight win-rate)
human_questions.recommendation: Gentle curve, high floor, labour-only throttle — matches the 'never a wall, paces the day' intent and leaves the LOCKED first-fight win-rate untouched; satiety still drains in combat and is managed via rest/eat between fights (§2.8c). Register the four magnitudes as §4.9 levers.

human_questions.question: How does a dented pillar's value return to its untouched highWater? The seasonal appraisal increments value only when seasonalScore > highWater; after a dent (HW unchanged) that condition never fires, so the §4.2.4 'self-heals via the appraisal / within 1-2 seasons' promise cannot work. Either (a) add an explicit below-high-water restore branch (a bounded maintenance award, which the 'no maintenance trickle' canon currently forbids — needs a scoped exception), or (b) drop the self-heal language and state a dent is recovered only by fresh recognized deed-jumps (active re-grind), guaranteeing an always-available repeatable deed per dentable pillar so recovery can't dead-end.
human_questions.context: 'Recoverable dent, never a wipe, self-heals' is canon (ADR D-015). The only un-gated heal path today is fresh achievement jumps, i.e. re-grinding — which contradicts 'self-heals as play resumes the appraisal' and risks a player who exhausted a tier's deed inventory being stuck below earned standing.
options: a-restore-branch: add a bounded below-HW restore each season (carve a scoped exception to the no-maintenance rule)
b-regrind-only: rewrite §4.2.4 to say dents heal only via fresh deeds; guarantee an always-available repeatable deed per dentable pillar
human_questions.recommendation: Option (a) — a small below-high-water restore branch that does NOT advance highWater preserves the 'self-heals, never a wipe' canon with a tightly-scoped exception; (b) risks dead-ending a tier whose authored deed inventory is spent.

human_questions.question: Define the durability wear/break model so M2b's deterministic reduce can apply it: (1) wear rate per combat hit / per labour action (constant or scaled by damage/tier); (2) at-zero behaviour (auto-unequip, function at base stats, or stay equipped contributing zero); (3) stat scaling (continuous falloff with durability, or full-until-break binary).
human_questions.context: §2.10/§6.4 store durability and prose says it 'wears', but no rule exists; M2b phase 2 requires reduce to apply wear immutably inside the deterministic core. An ad-hoc rule would be determinism-affecting.
options: simple-binary: fixed wear per action, auto-unequip at 0, full statMods until break (simplest, matches 'clear power step per tier')
continuous: durability-scaled statMods + degrade-to-base at 0 (more depth, complicates the §4.6 effectiveStats fold and tuning)
human_questions.recommendation: Binary (fixed wear, auto-unequip at 0, full-until-break) — simplest, most legible, avoids a stat branch in the deterministic combat fold; document in §2.10 with numbers as §4.9 levers.

human_questions.question: Resolve the persisted data-model gaps in §6.4 against the §2 type sketches: (a) how to store the estateWealth land/treasury/trade sub-engines the ≤⅓ clamp reads (nest on the pillar entry, a parallel subEngines map, or derive the pillar total from stored sub-engines); (b) sketch CombatEncounterState's fields (combatants/HP/statuses, rngCursor, subTickAccumulator, mobDefId) and unify the mid-fight verb-payload name (CombatAction vs CombatInterventionIntent); (c) declare equip's id space (drop EquipDefId → ItemId, or define EquipDefId = ItemId + the reduce() mapping); (d) does v1 support intra-line dialogue choices (add a choices[]/ChoiceId backing shape) or branch only via locksLineIds[] (drop choiceId from the intent)?
human_questions.context: §6.4 stores one scalar per pillar (no sub-engine field for the hardest invariant), never sketches the one must-persist mid-fight blob, and carries two undeclared id types (EquipDefId, ChoiceId) plus a verb-name drift — all load-bearing for save/determinism and the M2b clamp tests.
options: minimal-uniform: nest subEngines on estateWealth; sketch CombatEncounterState in §2.8(c); equip uses ItemId (drop EquipDefId); dialogue branches via locksLineIds only (drop choiceId)
feature-rich: parallel subEngine map; keep distinct EquipDefId with mapping; add intra-line choices[] + ChoiceId
human_questions.recommendation: Minimal: store subEngines (with per-strand high-water) on the estateWealth entry, sketch CombatEncounterState explicitly, collapse equipment to ItemId, and branch dialogue via locksLineIds[] (drop choiceId) unless intra-line choices are genuinely wanted in v1.

human_questions.question: For v1, do weather hazards and festivals carry MECHANICAL effects (needing §4 magnitudes AND a persisted home), or are they cosmetic-only with effects parked post-v1? If mechanical: add a bounded weatherMult to §4.7.1 + a combat-rate modifier to §4.6 (e.g. within ±10% so the locked first-fight/throughput bands hold), festival vendorRestock%/reputation-ip tables, a §4.9 lever entry, AND either store weather in GameState.clock or key it to a day-deterministic child RNG so reload can't perturb the main stream.
human_questions.context: §2.14/§6.4/§6.7/M5 type weather with labour/combat rateModifiers, schedule it, and reserve it an RNG sub-stream, yet §4 has zero magnitudes and no formula hook, and §6.4 gives the live per-day weather draw no persisted field (breaking byte-identical resume).
options: mechanical: add bounded magnitudes to §4.6/§4.7 + §4.9 lever + persist/day-key weather
cosmetic-v1: downgrade §2.14(e)/M5 to flavour-only tags + social beats, park modifiers post-v1 (weather then needs no §4 magnitudes and no persisted field beyond display)
human_questions.recommendation: If shipping the 'living world' pillar, go mechanical with bounded (±10%) modifiers + day-keyed deterministic weather; otherwise explicitly downgrade to cosmetic-v1 so §4 owes nothing — pick one rather than leaving it half-specified.

human_questions.question: Determinism policy for growth-curve powers: (a) mandate integer-power-by-repeated-multiplication for all r^n curves AND extend the §6.1 core lint to ban Math.pow/exp/log/trig (whitelisting Math.sqrt), making byte-identical replay hold across machines/builds; or (b) accept that byte-identical replay is guaranteed only within one JS-engine build and re-word the §6.1/§6.7/§6.12 promises and the qa-playtesting determinism claim accordingly.
human_questions.context: All growth curves use integer-exponent `^`; ECMAScript guarantees correct rounding only for +−×÷ and sqrt, so Math.pow can differ by a ULP across engines and a round() near x.5 flips an integer level/cost threshold, desyncing exported saves reloaded under a different engine. CI runs one engine, so the practical break is cross-engine save portability + an overclaimed promise.
options: a-strengthen: integer-pow helper + extend lint (cheap; preserves the strong cross-machine guarantee)
b-rescope: limit the guarantee to one engine build and reword the promises (no code constraint, weaker QA substrate)
human_questions.recommendation: (a) — every exponent is an integer so repeated multiplication is fully deterministic and nearly free; extending the lint closes the one cross-machine hole and keeps the LOCKED determinism principle honest.

human_questions.question: What storage-survival posture should v1 commit to for itch.io's cross-origin game iframe (third-party IndexedDB is partitioned on Chrome/Firefox, blocked-by-default on Safari/WebKit), and what must M7's done criteria require? The single autosave's only backup (base64 export) is opt-in.
human_questions.context: §6.8's 'robust, durable' single IndexedDB autosave can be silently wiped on Safari/private-mode, and M7's smoke test names no browser matrix — a 28.5h save loss is a real launch defect in a category no prior lens audited. (Note: itch fullscreen does NOT change the storage partition, so that mitigation is moot.)
options: matrix-only: add an explicit M7 done-criterion that autosave-persists-across-reload runs in the embedded itch iframe on Chromium AND WebKit/Safari
matrix+nudge: also promote base64 export to a first-class, periodically-nudged backup so a store wipe is never unrecoverable
full: add a load-time IndexedDB-writability probe that surfaces a recovery/import banner on failure, plus the above
human_questions.recommendation: matrix+nudge: make base64 export a periodically-nudged first-class backup (low cost, high payoff for a single-slot 28.5h game) AND add the WebKit/Safari + embedded-iframe line to M7's done criteria; the writability probe is a worthwhile stretch.

human_questions.question: Resolve two un-audited presentation/asset risks for v1. (A) Emoji are load-bearing aesthetic carriers but render as different per-OS artwork / tofu where no colour-emoji font exists, defeating the 'consistent woodblock, not AI-slop' pillar that one CSS filter can't rescue — bundle a controlled emoji font subset (reintroduces an asset pipeline), replace the load-bearing motifs with the already-permitted inline SVG, or accept variance and revisit at M6? (B) Audio is a real asset pipeline with no stated licensing/credits — use original/CC0 (no attribution), third-party CC-BY/OFL (add a Credits surface), or scope audio out of v1?
human_questions.context: D-013 locks TEXT+EMOJI+CSS and §2.21 makes emoji carry pillar/season/rarity identity (emoji are aria-hidden so meaning is carried by kanji/word — no functional break, purely aesthetic). Audio licensing on a public PWYW release is real legal exposure; the PRD is otherwise meticulous about provenance but silent here. Both sit in the deploy/asset-reality category no lens owned.
options: svg-motifs + original/CC0-audio: replace load-bearing emoji with inline SVG (zero asset pipeline, full control) and use original or CC0 audio (no attribution needed)
bundle-font + cc-by-audio: ship an emoji-font subset and CC-BY audio with a Credits/About surface
park-to-M6: accept emoji variance and treat both as M6 polish, naming them so they aren't forgotten
human_questions.recommendation: Replace the load-bearing period motifs (pillar/season/rarity marks) with inline SVG and reserve Unicode emoji for cosmetically-harmless roles; use original/CC0 audio (or add a one-line Credits surface if any third-party asset is used). Add a cross-platform emoji-rendering check to the capture-game-states sweep.

human_questions.question: Authorial naming/cast hygiene (all low, several touch LOCKED canon). (a) Rename the non-locked T3 clerk Naozane off 'Nao-' to separate him from heir Naoyuki, and the herbalist 'Obaa Sato' off 'Sa-' to separate from Sayo? (b) The Kuroiwa/Kurosawa stem overlap — rename the antagonist (touches locked roster + ~10 uses) or accept as authentic? (c) Re-glyph AGI 体→敏 (or 技) so it matches agility rather than 'body'? (d) Add a §1.8 row for recurring T4 spine figure Konoe (and a scope note for personifications/forward NPCs)? (e) Kanbei's v1-seeded 'player-influenceable détente/washout' has no beat or host system — author a lightweight T2.7 beat, reword to a static flavour card, or defer to T3+?
human_questions.context: §1.8 is the cited cast concordance anchor; the collisions co-occur in the same scenes in a text-dense log UI. Most are mild and several names are LOCKED, so renames reopen locked decisions — these are taste calls only the human arbitrates.
options: minimal: rename only non-locked Naozane (+ Obaa Sato), re-glyph AGI→敏, add Konoe row, reword Kanbei to flavour-card
leave-as-is: accept all as period-authentic (Kuro-/Sa-/-yuki/-emon) and add only a §1.8 scope note
full: also nudge a locked -yuki/Sō- name (reopens locked canon)
human_questions.recommendation: Minimal: rename the two non-locked names (Naozane, Obaa Sato), re-glyph AGI→敏, add a Konoe row, and reword Kanbei to a static flavour card (drop 'player-influenceable'); leave the locked names and the authentic -emon cluster alone.

human_questions.question: Resolve the diegetic-register and climax-duplication prose issues (§3). The load-bearing one: the guaranteed G6 spine line and the optional O5 Origin capstone share four near-verbatim sentences and fire at the same moment, and the G6 spine already speaks the 'Tahei' naming that §3.6.2 reserves for the Origin track — who owns the naming? Also: re-cast the V7 'The room is clean' meta-line in concrete estate terms; strip the two T1 mechanics/UI parentheticals from diegetic copy; re-aim the G0 dream-ache at the MC.
human_questions.context: O5 is meant to DEEPEN G6, not echo it; as written an Origin-completing player reads a doubled log entry, and a non-Origin player still receives the naming the spec reserves for O5 — a payoff-ownership contradiction at the game's emotional peak. The other three are low tone-polish on illustrative spec copy.
options: naming-on-Origin: G6 keeps only the Otsuru truth-reveal, drops 'Tahei'; naming lands solely at O5 (honors the rule; non-Origin players never reclaim the name — possibly the intended cost)
naming-on-spine: keep 'Tahei' on the guaranteed spine and rewrite O5 to add what G6 can't (present-day family witnessing, reunions completing) — relaxes the 'naming is Origin-gated' rule
human_questions.recommendation: naming-on-spine: keep the naming on the guaranteed G6 spine (every player should reclaim the name) and rewrite O5 to genuinely deepen rather than restate; apply the trivial tone fixes (V7 concrete recast, strip the two parentheticals, re-aim the G0 ache). Confirm whether 'clean your room' is ever meant to reach the player.

human_questions.question: Harden the single-source-of-truth doc surfaces (all low, no live drift today): (a) Name's per-deed-cap band-top (10K/80K) conflicts with its seasonal normalizer TIER_REF_NAME (15K/140K) and the cap formula cites a §4.1 gate Name lacks in v1 — pick one Name band-top and fix the '(= its §4.1 gate threshold)' gloss; (b) the per-deed cap (0.04·band-top) and the four-pillar table are hand-restated in 4-6 places — strip to formula/single generated table, tag as 'derived', or add a §6.6 verifier assertion; (c) §6.6/§7 emit balance tables to docs/balance/ while CLAUDE.md says docs/content/ — reconcile the path.
human_questions.context: These are latent drift traps and a cross-doc path contradiction the 'generate, don't duplicate' convention targets; currently consistent but each requires lockstep hand-edits on any retune.
options: lightweight: tag duplicated numbers 'derived (not an independent lever)', pick Name's band-top, and update the CLAUDE.md path line to the PRD's two-directory split
generated: promote the pillar/cap/conversion tables to generated docs/ outputs + a verifier assertion that every deed base ≤ 0.04·band-top
human_questions.recommendation: Lightweight now (tag derived values, choose Name's §4.0 display band-top + fix the gate gloss, update CLAUDE.md to docs/balance/ + docs/content/), add the §6.6 verifier assertion when balance.ts lands; full generation is premature for content that isn't yet game data.

human_questions.question: Are these two genre-convention surfaces in scope for lean v1? (a) Bulk affordances — sell-N/sell-all/sell-to-floor on the broker and craft-N on stations — and critically, how does the market-saturation damper apply across a bulk sale (progressive per-unit walk-down vs single pre-sale price)? (b) An in-game changelog/'What's new' panel matching both inspirations.
human_questions.context: Both are near-universal idle/incremental conventions, but TRADE is a deliberately hard-capped ≤⅓ minor strand and koku is not stack-sold, so bulk payoff is limited to trade goods; itch.io provides native devlogs as the conventional out-of-game changelog channel. The damper-vs-bulk rule is a genuine undecided balance invariant regardless of UI.
options: decide-damper-only: settle the saturation-damper bulk-application rule (balance invariant) and treat a quantity stepper as an optional implementation nicety; rely on itch devlogs for changelog
add-both: ship bulk controls + an in-game changelog panel sourced from a docs/ data file
park: leave both out of v1, name the changelog as a post-launch item
human_questions.recommendation: Decide the damper bulk-application rule now (progressive per-unit walk-down, so the damper stays legible) as it's a balance invariant; treat bulk UI as an implementation nicety and rely on itch.io devlogs for the changelog given lean-v1 discipline.

cross_cutting_risks: The T0 economic spine's own proofs are internally invalid: the Estate gate is unreachable as itemized (trade deeds that can't exist in T0) and the koku held-vs-produced accounting is self-contradictory, so the affordability/soft-lock guarantees rounds 1-2 relied on are unproven. This is the single highest-leverage systemic theme.

Pervasive 'declared in §2, deferred to §4, never delivered' pattern: rung-meter accrual, soft-stamina throttle magnitudes + its combat applicability, dent self-heal, durability wear, and weather/festival modifiers are all type-backed and milestone-scheduled but lack the rule or numbers needed to implement and test them — directly blocking M1/M2b/M5 and undermining the single-source-of-truth contract.

The persisted data model (§6.4) is systematically narrower than the §2 type sketches: no field for the estateWealth sub-engines the ≤⅓ invariant reads, no CombatEncounterState shape for the one must-persist mid-fight blob, and no home for live weather — each threatens the save/byte-identical-resume and the machine-checked invariants.

Deploy-target reality (itch.io) was never audited by any lens across three rounds: cross-origin-iframe storage partitioning can silently wipe the single 28.5h autosave, cross-platform emoji variance defeats the anti-AI-slop aesthetic pillar, audio has no licensing/credits, and the zip layout breaks the upload — a whole category of real launch defects.

Over-claimed guarantees vs their enforcement: the cross-machine 'byte-identical replay' promise is contradicted by un-linted Math.pow in every growth curve; the 'robust/durable' save claim is inverted by the locked host; the 'no asset pipeline' claim ignores audio. Promises and their guardrails are drifting apart.

Documentation drift / SSOT erosion: the §4.9 'complete' tuning dashboard omits whole lever families (§4.3 conversion weights, §4.6.1 combat constants), derived numbers are hand-restated in 4-6 places, the pillar table is duplicated, and a CLAUDE.md vs PRD output-path conflict — all latent maintenance traps on a doc that bills itself as single-source-of-truth.

Authorial-voice register leaks: design-meta shorthand ('clean your room'), mechanics, and UI instructions surface in diegetic event-log copy, and the emotional-climax beat is duplicated with a payoff-ownership contradiction — small individually but collectively erode the 'grounded Edo, not AI-doc filler' tone bar.
synthesis.coverage_notes: "Round-3 verified findings concentrated in: T0 economic proofs (sim-t0 lens), the §2-vs-§6 data model, the number spine / levers index, naming/prose, and — newly — the deploy target. Items NOT yet verified or modelled and that remain open: (1) the held-koku per-rung trajectory is asserted but never computed, so E2@R6 soft-lock is unresolved pending Q29; (2) weather/festival magnitudes and persistence are entirely absent (Q35); (3) the rung-meter accrual law and soft-stamina/durability numbers are undefined (Q30/Q31/Q33) — no balance.ts exists yet to cross-check, so these are spec-only judgements; (4) cross-platform emoji fidelity and itch-iframe storage have NOT been empirically tested (the capture-game-states sweep samples one platform); (5) audio provenance is unstated. The completeness critic's recommended ROUND-4 TARGET is the deploy-target / host-and-browser reality matrix that no lens has owned: an actual itch.io cross-origin-iframe build smoke-tested on Chromium AND WebKit/Safari for IndexedDB save survival, cross-platform emoji rendering, bundle size against the <5s load budget, and audio licensing/credits — plus the adjacent un-owned categories of accessibility/screen-reader behaviour of the text-only UI, save-schema migration robustness, and legal/attribution surface. Secondary round-4 candidate: a held-koku ledger simulation to close the T0 affordability/soft-lock question empirically rather than by re-itemization."
